findings.detail: The webhook compares the computed HMAC-SHA256 to the attacker-supplied Stripe-Signature value with Go's built-in `==` on the hex strings. `==` short-circuits on the first differing byte, so comparison time leaks how many leading bytes match — a classic timing oracle for forging a valid signature byte by byte.
findings.remediation: Compare with crypto/subtle.ConstantTimeCompare (or hmac.Equal), which runs in time independent of the data.
findings.chain_note: With the signing secret already leaked (F-SEC-001), the attacker does not even need the oracle — but the timing-unsafe compare is independently exploitable and is the code-level defect to fix.
